title: NA-Non ULIP
word_text:
SEO Insertion on What Is Meant By Pure Endowment? - Article Page
Keyword density and no. of target keywords has been mentioned as comments in the beginning
Suggested additional anchor texts are marked as comments in Yellow. (If required)
Keywords: pure endowment policy, pure endowment plan, pure endowment
Body Content:
A pure endowment policy is an ideal instrument to maintain financial discipline. By availing a pure endowment plan, you can ensure that you are saving in order to have a corpus for any future financial needs that may arise.
With pure endowment, you receive a payout on maturity of your policy period. However, it is important to note that while other life insurance policies offer the maturity amount to your family and dependents in case of your untimely demise, the same is not true with a pure endowment policy. To avail the sum assured at the end of your pure endowment policy, the policyholder will need to be alive.
It is always recommended to begin planning for your retirement early on in life. One key reason is that you never know what tomorrow might bring. If you have plans that include realising your life’s dreams such travelling or vacationing with your family, you can never start planning too early.
A pure endowment policy is one such plan that enables you to realise your life’s dreams. If you are also concerned about ensuring that your family is well looked after once you are no longer around, you should opt for a life insurance plan alongside a pure endowment policy. While the life insurance plan will enable your family to take care of their expenses, the pure endowment plan can be utilised to meet your regular expenses once you have retired from regular work and no longer have a regular salary coming in.
What Does Pure Endowment Mean?
There are a few aspects related to how pure endowment policies work that you should know about before you invest in one.
Maturity:The endowment policy is designed to offer you funds for meeting your financial goals later in life. As a result, you can only get the amount guaranteed if you are alive. Some insurers also offer bonuses along with the guaranteed amount, which is calculated mostly at a 5% rate of interest. However, it is important to note that you get almost no coverage at all as compared to a pure life insurance policy, which is why you are usually advised to get the latter along with a pure endowment plan.
Savings:While the above point is important, it is also important to remember that a pure endowment policy works as a great savings tool. By investing in an endowment policy, you can receive a guaranteed sum at the end of your policy period, which can help you meet a range of financial goals. Additionally, if you invest in an endowment policy throughout your life, you can reap rich dividends at the end of the maturity period, which could allow you to live out your sunset years in the best way possible.
Payout at Maturity:A pure endowment plan assures you returns at the end of the maturity period if you live until the end of the term. While usually the entire amount is paid in a lump sum manner, it could include some bonus payments alongside the sum agreed upon.
No Beneficiaries:There are no benefits for beneficiaries when an individual invests in a pure endowment plan. While a life insurance plan ensures that beneficiaries are looked after in case the policyholder runs into unfortunate events, the same is not true for pure endowment policies. The only amount received through a pure endowment plan is the guaranteed sum, which is paid out to the policyholder if they live until the end of the maturity period.
Legality:In many states across the nation, pure endowment plans are illegal unless accompanied by a traditional life insurance policy that pays benefits to nominees mentioned in the policy once the policyholder dies. With a pure endowment policy, you might be required to additionally avail a term insurance plan to ensure your policy is legal. In case the policyholder is not alive at the end of the endowment plan, they will not receive the guaranteed sum, which is usually calculated as being equal to the amount of premiums paid.
Regular Premium Payments:In order to ensure that you receive the amount you have been guaranteed since the beginning of your pure endowment policy, the policyholder is required to pay premiums until the very end of the policy term. If the policyholder stops paying the premiums in the middle of the term of the pure endowment policy or dies in an untimely manner, there will be no benefits to the policy at all.
Conclusion
A pure endowment plan can be useful for meeting a host of goals. With the returns from a pure endowment policy, you can opt to pay the fees for your children’s higher education, pay for their lavish weddings, or even travel to an exotic location that you have always wanted to with your entire family. However, it is important to remember that a pure endowment plan does not negate the need for a traditional life insurance policy.
Regardless of whether you opt for a term insurance plan or a permanent life insurance coverage policy, you will be required to have at least one of them. Life insurance policies ensure that your family is looked after even if you are no longer around. It allows them to pay off any debts and immediately meet any expenses that crop up. The benefits of a life insurance policy are unmatched by pure endowment plans.
